triggers toggle now.

Replace verbose "Cliquer pour modifier..." with quiet type-specific
indicators: dash for text/number, persistent icons for date/phone/
email/location, muted User icon without text, "—" for empty selects.

design: add dark/light theme system with next-themes

Install next-themes, add ThemeProvider + ThemeToggle, define CSS
design tokens for both modes in globals.css, migrate ~1500 hardcoded
colors to semantic tokens across all components. Sidebar is light in
light mode, dark navy in dark mode. All color-coded elements (badges,
file types, status indicators) use dark: variants.

Mission-focused upgrades: kill counter, decapitation tracker, data fixes

  counter (16) as the FIRST stats in the top bar — red glow, pulsing
  border. This is what makes TangoDown different.

- Kill Board: Now the DEFAULT tab on Lion's Eye (not sitrep). The
  kill board is the product.

- Org Decapitation Tracker: Shows leadership attrition per terror org
  with percentage bars — Hamas, Hezbollah, ISIS, Al-Qaeda, etc.
  Big "117 TOTAL HVTs ELIMINATED" counter with global attrition %.

- Data fix: Removed Hashem Safieddine and Mohammed Sinwar from active
  list (they were in both eliminated AND active — data integrity issue).

commit.message:
fix: use conservative 1 Mbps initial BWE instead of hard bitrate cap

Replace the 10 Mbps hard cap with a low initial BWE estimate (1 Mbps).
This activates mediasoup's pacer from the start, forcing it to spread
packets evenly. The BWE will ramp up based on real transport-cc feedback
from the client, reaching full speed without any artificial ceiling.

commit.message:
Add Paychex Labor Cost CSV parser and simplify Payroll upload to one file

parse_paychex_labor_cost() handles the headerless Paychex export format:
derives gross_pay from hourly_rate x hours, calculates overtime (>40hrs),
sets week_start = pay_date - 6 days, spreads pay evenly across 7 days for
daily_labor. Returns (weekly_payroll_df, daily_labor_df) from one file.
Payroll page now shows a single uploader instead of two separate files.

audit logging, transaction limits, password reset flow.

commit.message:
Add InputSanitizer for LLM input normalization

Removes invisible characters, normalizes Unicode (NFC), strips control
chars, replaces homoglyphs, normalizes whitespace, and limits length.
Zero-dependency defense against character-level smuggling attacks.
39 tests, all passing.

Move GitHub integration to backend, add live progress & inline PR reviews

  /api/gh/* endpoints (repos, PRs, status, PR review)

- Upgrade post_pr_review_comments to post inline comments with the
  GitHub Reviews API (severity icons, suggestions, code blocks)
- Add avatar_url to GhStatusResponse, stargazers_count/private to GhRepo
- Add React Query hooks for GitHub operations (useGhStatus, useGhRepos,
  useGhPrs, useStartPrReview)
- Add ProgressCallback/ProgressUpdate to review pipeline for live
  per-file progress tracking
- Add ProgressBanner and EventPanel components to ReviewView
- Frontend shows partial results during scan (comments stream in)

commit.message:
docs: add WebMCP section and complete tool list in README (#22)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(cdt): add machine-verifiable acceptance criteria to plan template (#144)

* feat(cdt): add machine-verifiable acceptance criteria to plan template

- Add VERIFY:-prefixed acceptance criteria section to plan template
- Add boundaries section with green/yellow/red scope tiers
- Update architect prompt with testable assertion writing rule
- Update dev-workflow parse step and final verification to check VERIFY: items
- Update testing strategy to reference acceptance criteria
- Document template drift pattern in learnings

Fixes #113

* fix: address PR review comments

- Clarify VERIFY: format as checkbox `- [ ] VERIFY:` in architect rule
- Update Parse Plan extraction to include checkbox-style criteria
- Add concrete example to performance target placeholder
- Forward acceptance criteria to code-tester and qa-tester in Step 6

* fix: add VERIFY failure recovery path and accept checked checkboxes

- Add failure guidance to Final Verification: document failures, message
  developer, re-run tests, re-verify
- Accept `- [x] VERIFY:` (checked checkbox) in Parse Plan extraction

* fix: mark passing VERIFY items as checked in plan file

Explicitly instruct the Lead to tick `- [x]` for passing acceptance
criteria during Final Verification, closing the checkbox lifecycle loop.

* fix: address PR review comments

* fix: reset failing VERIFY items to unchecked on re-verification

Make checkbox updates symmetric — previously only passing items were
marked [x], leaving stale checked state on items that fail during
session resume re-verification.

* fix: address PR review comments

commit.message:
Fix TS error: don't pass boolean to void useLike mutate()

useLike's mutationFn takes no variables, so TanStack Query infers
TVariables=void. Passing liked (boolean) to mutate() fails type check.
Since useLike is a toggle, the boolean argument is unused anyway — fix
both ArticleCard and ArticleFullView to call mutate() with no args.

commit.message:
fix: final audit fixes — recovery DB save, memory leaks, FR accents, touch targets

WalkMode.jsx:
- Recovery now saves to DailyLog DB (was display-only, data lost)
- clearInterval before creating new timer (prevents double-speed)
- startGPS guard clears existing watchPosition (prevents GPS leak)
- setInterval 500ms → 1000ms (halves re-renders, saves battery)
- Persist pausedRef immediately on resume (crash safety)
- "promenade" → "balade" (terminology consistency)
- Mood emoji padding p-2 → p-2.5, tags py-1 → py-2 (touch targets)
- Pause/Stop gap-4 → gap-6 (prevents accidental taps)

Code cleanup:
- DogTrophiesRow: rename shadowed variable `current` → `curRun`
- badgeUtils: remove unused `todayLog` variable

French accents (8 fixes):
- BadgeTeaser: Légende, Coach débutant, Première balade, Régulier, à débloquer
- TrackerHistory: "Aucune balade enregistrée", "Activité"
- ActiveProgramCards: "À faire", "À éviter"

- Goal suggestion chips py-1.5 → py-2.5

commit.message:
Fix social post export clipping — render at full resolution, scale for display

Root cause: html2canvas was capturing a 520px preview div and trying to scale it
up, causing content clipping and quality loss.

Fix:
- CanvasPreview now accepts exportWidth/exportHeight and renders the div at
  FULL platform resolution (e.g. 1080x1080, 1200x630)
- In SocialGenerator, the full-size div is wrapped in a CSS transform: scale()
  container for display only — the outer clip box is displayW x displayH
- overflow:hidden is ONLY on the display wrapper, never on the export div
- html2canvas options: explicit width/height at export size, scale:2 for retina,
  scrollX/Y=0, windowWidth/Height matching export size
- Await document.fonts.ready and all img.onload before capturing
- DISPLAY_MAX_WIDTH = 540px; previewScale = min(1, 540/exportW)
  e.g. Facebook (1200): scale=0.45, Instagram (1080): scale=0.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Disable Gradio analytics to fix APP_STARTING hang

GRADIO_ANALYTICS_ENABLED=False prevents Gradio from pinging its analytics
server at startup, which times out and blocks the health check response.
Also set GRADIO_SERVER_NAME and GRADIO_SERVER_PORT as env vars so they
are guaranteed regardless of launch() arguments.
